response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine AI-native experience (MCP code generators, agentic workflow tooling at re:cinq), strong TypeScript, and real high-throughput systems exposure from Sociomantic's microservices handling millions of requests per second. However, the role demands strong Python — which is absent from his resume entirely — his distributed-systems work is several years old and in DLang, and there is little evidence of directing technical strategy or managing cross-functional stakeholders at staff scope. Worth a conversation if Python depth exists undisclosed, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP generators, agentic frameworks) matches the AI-native environment
High-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second)
Strong software craftsmanship record: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authored, workshops and mentoring
concerns: No Python anywhere on the resume, a must-have skill listed as 'strong' for this role
Most recent distributed-systems work ended in 2020 and was in DLang, not the required stack
Thin evidence of staff-level technical strategy, stakeholder coordination, and operational/on-call ownership
